pattern: Repeatedly restarting a leftmost-pair scan after every merge is wasteful busywork — a single left-to-right pass that, for each new character, looks back at most k already-kept characters and drops it if a match is found there reproduces exactly the same final string as the repeated-restart process, because the merge order never actually changes the end result.
hints: Simulating the process literally (find the leftmost close pair, delete the right one, repeat from the start) works but re-scans from scratch after every single merge — think about whether you can decide, in one left-to-right pass, whether each character survives.

Keep a running list of characters that have survived so far. When you reach the next character of the original string, it only needs to be compared against the last k *surviving* characters — if one of them matches, this character merges away (is dropped) instead of being appended.

Because a dropped character never appears in the surviving list, the 'distance' for future comparisons is always measured against the current (already-collapsed) positions, not the original string's positions — a stack (or list you only ever append to / peek into) is enough, and it reproduces the same final string as repeatedly restarting the scan.

edge_inputs.description: a long run of the same character with k=1 chains down to a single character, one merge at a time
same run of characters but k equal to the string length — still collapses to one character
all distinct characters — no pair is ever close enough, string is unchanged
alternating two-letter pattern with k=1 — no two equal characters are ever adjacent, so nothing merges despite many repeats overall
max length (100), all one character, k=1 — full chain collapse
max length (100), all one character, k equal to the full length
a repeated character pair separated by a different character, small k — only the closer-together repeat should merge

stress.description: random strings over a small alphabet (to force frequent merges) with random k, seeded
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    letters = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [s, k]
